title: Lumen — a from-scratch path tracer
description: A physically based path tracer built from scratch in TypeScript. NEWEST (11.0) — REAL METALS FROM MEASURED COMPLEX IOR: a conductor's colour is not an RGB tint but a spectral reflectance set by its complex refractive index n̄(λ)=η(λ)−ik(λ), so Lumen now carries measured η/k spectra (gold/silver/copper from Johnson & Christy, aluminium from Rakić, iron & chromium) and evaluates the EXACT unpolarised conductor Fresnel at the path's committed hero wavelength — the same machinery that disperses glass — so a metal's hue emerges spectrally: gold rims warm, copper reads red, silver stays bright-neutral and aluminium leans faintly blue, with the correct grazing desaturation that Schlick-from-RGB can never capture. The complex-IOR Fresnel rides on every existing lobe (single-scatter GGX, anisotropic brushed metal, AND the Kulla–Conty multiscatter compensation) through one shared FresnelSpec, gated behind an optional spectrum field so the prior 55 proofs stay bit-for-bit green. New Metals of the World scene + six proofs — R∈[0,1] & grazing→1, the k→0 limit reducing EXACTLY to the dielectric Fresnel, the textbook metal colours, a furnace render reconstructing the measured RGB head-on, sampler↔pdf↔weight MIS consistency, and spectral multiscatter energy bounded by the hemispherical-average reflectance F̄. Also (10.0) — a physically based MATERIAL SYSTEM that makes the surfaces as rigorous as the light transport: ENERGY-CONSERVING ROUGH METAL via a Kulla–Conty multiple-scattering compensation lobe (driven by the GGX directional albedo E(μ,α) Monte-Carlo–integrated into a table at start-up) so rough gold stops darkening to grey and stays bright and saturated — proven in-app to reflect ≈1 in a white furnace with compensation and measurably less without; ANISOTROPIC (brushed/milled) METAL with two GGX roughness axes in a rotatable tangent frame so the highlight stretches into a streak, reciprocity and energy preserved exactly; OREN–NAYAR rough-diffuse for chalk/clay/plaster/the-moon flat-matte look; and CLEAR-COAT layered materials (glazed ceramic, lacquer, car paint) — a GGX dielectric coat over a Lambert/Oren–Nayar base, the two lobes sampled by Fresnel weight with a combined pdf so the stack conserves energy AND stays MIS-consistent (the new BSDFs match BDPT pixel-for-pixel in the verify suite). Three new showcase scenes — Brushed Metal, Rough Conductors (a single-scatter vs multiscatter split), and Ceramics & Clay — plus six new correctness proofs. It carries FOUR selectable light-transport integrators: a unidirectional Monte-Carlo path tracer, a full bidirectional path tracer (BDPT) — Veach–Guibas camera×light vertex connections weighted by balance-heuristic multiple importance sampling, which renders indirect-lit scenes (an emitter hidden in a cove) at a fraction of the noise — primary-sample-space Metropolis light transport (PSSMLT), a Markov chain run over the path tracer's own random-number stream (Kelemen mutation + Metropolis-Hastings acceptance, bootstrap normalisation, expected-value splatting, parallel chains across the worker pool) that locks onto the hardest-to-find light and refines the whole frame at once — the path tracer is reused verbatim as the contribution function because the Metropolis sampler simply IS the RNG — AND stochastic progressive photon mapping (SPPM), which runs the transport BACKWARDS to resolve the CAUSTICS the other three sample terribly (light focused through glass onto a floor — a light→specular→diffuse path next-event estimation can't connect through the lens): photons are emitted from the area lights (power-sampled to equal flux), refracted through the specular geometry and deposited at per-pixel measurement points via an exact CSR spatial hash, while a jittered camera pass each iteration places those points and the per-point gather radius is shrunk on the Hachisuka schedule R²←R²·(N+αM)/(N+M) so the density-estimation bias provably vanishes and the estimate converges. Because all four estimators solve the same rendering equation, the in-app verification suite proves they converge to the SAME image (checking PSSMLT and SPPM against the path tracer on both global brightness and spatial distribution), that SPPM resolves a finite focused caustic the others miss, that the photon-gather grid returns exactly the in-radius points vs brute force, and that the MIS weights form an exact partition of unity. The photon mapper is also SPECTRAL — a photon commits a random hero wavelength on its first dispersive hit so the caustic it focuses through glass fans into a RAINBOW (proven to conserve total energy while spreading into colour) — and DAYLIGHT-complete: the sun is itself a photon emitter (photons leave a disc sized to the scene's bounding sphere as a parallel beam), so daylight scenes get photon-mapped SUN CAUSTICS, with the distant-light flux normalisation proven against the path tracer. Showcase scenes include a Caustic Pool — a sine-ripple water surface (analytic smooth normals) throwing the shifting bright filaments of a swimming pool. Plus participating media (fog/smoke/cloud) with Henyey-Greenstein scattering and volumetric god rays, thin-film interference for soap-bubble/oil-slick iridescence, smooth triangle meshes (icosphere, lathe & OBJ import) with barycentric-interpolated normals, a SAH bounding volume hierarchy, GGX microfacet BSDFs with VNDF sampling, smooth and frosted dielectrics, spectral dispersion (prism rainbows), Beer-Lambert volumetric absorption, procedural textures, an analytic Preetham daylight sky whose sun is a sampled light, next-event estimation with multiple importance sampling, scrambled-Halton low-discrepancy sampling, adaptive variance-guided sampling with a live noise heatmap, a multi-threaded Web Worker pool, ACES tone mapping, and an edge-avoiding À-Trous denoiser — global illumination on the CPU, in your browser. The participating media are now HETEROGENEOUS too: procedural 3D fBm density fields (cumulus clouds, rising smoke plumes, exponential ground-fog layers) traced unbiasedly with the null-collision estimators — DELTA TRACKING (Woodcock free-flight sampling against a constant majorant, accepting a real scatter with probability σ(x)/σ̄) for in-volume collisions and RATIO TRACKING for shadow-ray transmittance — so a cloud self-shadows into soft greys, flares a forward-scattering silver lining toward the sun, and breaks light into real volumetric beams, all from one continuously varying field, with five new proofs (delta tracking reproduces e^(−σL), ratio tracking matches a fine quadrature of a varying optical depth, and a heterogeneous pure-scattering volume conserves energy exactly). Media can also EMIT light volumetrically — at a real collision the path collects (1−albedo)·Lₑ of self-radiance, so a heterogeneous field glows brightest in its dense core (a soft, physically integrated fireball, not a billboard), proven to obey the emission–absorption law (1−e^(−σ_t·chord))·Lₑ; see the glowing Ember scene.
